ai_strategy_lead.name: Deepika Chalemela
ai_strategy_lead.title: Chief Information Officer and Chair, UTA AI Council

documents.title: Artificial Intelligence at UTA (central AI hub, resources, people, initiatives)
Researcher Guidance for the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Research
Use of Generative Artificial Intelligence (Research Administration policy)

ai_strategy.summary: UTA's AI strategy is coordinated through a central 'Artificial Intelligence at UTA' hub (ai.uta.edu) and governed by a multi-office AI Council chaired by the CIO. The university frames AI adoption around three ideas: ethical/responsible use, hands-on enablement for faculty and staff, and research impact in priority domains (smart agriculture, drug discovery, engineering, big data). Rather than publishing a single board-approved strategy document, UTA has stitched together guideline packs for instruction, research, and operations, an AI Learning Hub built on LinkedIn Learning/Microsoft content, and proof-of-concept deployments (nebulaONE, CloudForce travel assistant) run through the Office of Information Technology's innovation team.

key_provisions: UTA GenAI Guidelines for Faculty (2025) supply four syllabus language options spanning prohibited to unrestricted AI use
Only publicly classified data may be entered into public GenAI tools; FERPA, health data, proprietary/confidential data are prohibited
Researchers must disclose GAI use in proposals and publications and remain responsible for all outputs
GAI cannot be used for NSF or NIH peer review per sponsor rules
UTA requires human oversight; outputs must be verified against reliable sources to catch hallucinations
Procurement of GAI tools must route through legal, information security, audit, and procurement review
'Guiding Principles for use of GAI' emphasize ethical, transparent use and bias mitigation
ai_policy.summary: UTA runs a comprehensive but faculty-flexible policy stack. The 2025 GenAI Guidelines for Faculty give instructors four syllabus language options ranging from prohibited to unrestricted use, while parallel research and staff guidance prohibits entering FERPA, health, or proprietary data into public tools and requires disclosure when GAI is used in proposals or publications. Procurement of any GAI tool must clear legal, security, audit, and procurement review.

bodies.name: UTA AI Council
bodies.scope: Chaired by the CIO with membership from Research Administration, Legal, Academic Affairs, IT Planning, and the Chief Data and Analytics Officer; sets institutional AI direction, reviews policy, and owns the ai.uta.edu resource hub

bodies.name: UTA AI Advisory Team
bodies.scope: Cross-functional operational team with members from OIT Research Support, Information Security, the library, University Analytics, Faculty Senate, CRTLE, Faculty Success, and Employee Engagement; advises the AI Council on day-to-day implementation

bodies.name: OIT Innovation team
bodies.scope: Runs proof-of-concept pipelines, AI governance intake processes, and campus AI deployments (nebulaONE pilot, CloudForce travel assistant, IRB search agent)

bodies.name: Center for Research on Teaching and Learning Excellence (CRTLE)
bodies.scope: Faculty development around AI in teaching; assembled CRTLE AI lending library and co-hosts UT System AI webinar series

governance_structure.summary: Governance is distributed but coordinated. A CIO-chaired AI Council sets policy and direction, an AI Advisory Team handles operational detail, and OIT's innovation group executes pilots. Faculty development flows through CRTLE, which participates in the UT System's AI webinar series and maintains an AI-in-education lending library.

initiatives.title: Smart Agriculture Research Center (SARC) with USDA-ARS
initiatives.description: UTA opened a federally partnered AI research center in August 2025 (formal ribbon-cutting February 2026) that applies machine learning to agricultural grand challenges including plant disease prediction, soil health modeling, crop resilience forecasting, highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPAI) outbreak modeling, and livestock/poultry health monitoring. Co-directed by mathematics professor Jianzhong Su and CARIDA director Gautam Das.
initiatives.significance: Puts UTA in a lead role on USDA-aligned AI-for-agriculture work, with >$5.5M of connected external research investment and an annual 20-25 student summer research program.

investment.funding_source: USDA Agricultural Research Service and related external awards
investment.description: Over $5.5M in external research investment tied to UTA faculty and USDA-ARS collaborators

collaborations.description: Joint center pairing UTA faculty/students with USDA-ARS scientists on machine-learning-driven agricultural problems

initiatives.policy_implications: Aligns campus AI research with federal sustainability priorities and extends UTA's institutional gateway role for USDA partnerships

initiatives.title: nebulaONE AI Teaching Assistant Pilot
initiatives.description: A campus-wide proof-of-concept deploying nebulaONE (an AI interface powered by ChatGPT-4o with support for multiple LLMs including Gemini and Meta models) to more than 20 faculty members across the Fall 2025 semester. Each instance is customized with syllabi, lecture materials, and assignments so the assistant delivers course-specific responses and, in some cases, points students to resources rather than handing out full answers.
initiatives.significance: Tests a path toward course-embedded AI tutoring that could scale to Canvas, positioning UTA as an early R1 mover on per-course AI co-pilots.

investment.funding_source: UTA Office of Information Technology innovation budget

collaborations.description: UTA Office of Information Technology Innovation team collaborating with the nebulaONE platform and underlying LLM vendors

initiatives.policy_implications: Pilot testing informs future Canvas integration and campus-wide scale decisions; faculty are opting in with course-specific guardrails

initiatives.title: $2.1M Supercomputing Research Hub
initiatives.description: UTA launched a $2.1 million supercomputing hub in summer 2025 to support data-intensive AI, biomedical imaging, genomics, energy modeling, infrastructure design, and environmental modeling. Built by the College of Engineering and the Office of the VP for Research and Innovation, operationalized by OIT's Edward Gonzales.
initiatives.significance: Gives UTA researchers on-campus high-performance compute rather than routing everything through TACC at UT Austin, expanding AI-for-science capacity across the campus.

investment.funding_source: UTA College of Engineering and Office of the VP for Research and Innovation
investment.description: $2.1M investment to build dedicated supercomputing hub

collaborations.description: Routing to broader research networks via LEARN

initiatives.title: UTA GenAI Guidelines for Faculty (2025, Approved)
initiatives.description: Formal faculty guidelines approved in 2025 that advocate a balanced, SLO-aligned approach to GenAI in instruction and supply four syllabus policy options (from prohibited to unrestricted). Accompanies separate guidance for research and staff use, forming UTA's de facto comprehensive AI policy stack.
initiatives.significance: Gives instructors a consistent menu of syllabus language while preserving academic freedom, a model that UT System peers have referenced.
initiatives.policy_implications: Standardizes four syllabus options so students can read across courses; sits atop research and data-handling guardrails

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot Deployment Across UT System (UTA users)
initiatives.description: Microsoft Copilot is available at no additional cost to all UTA faculty, staff, and students under a UT System-wide commercial data-protection agreement with Microsoft. Paid 'Copilot for Microsoft 365' seats (deep Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook/Teams integration) can be requested through ServiceNow at $360 per fiscal year.
initiatives.significance: Gives tens of thousands of UTA users a sanctioned, FERPA-aware generative AI tool and signals that Microsoft is UTA's default enterprise AI vendor.

investment.funding_source: UT System enterprise agreement; premium seats paid by department
investment.description: Free baseline Copilot chat; $360/year per seat for Copilot for Microsoft 365 add-on

collaborations.description: System-level enterprise agreement with campus-level opt-in for premium features

tools_and_vendors.name: Copilot for Microsoft 365
tools_and_vendors.role: In-app AI in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams

initiatives.policy_implications: Data-protection terms let faculty use Copilot with lower-sensitivity data; public/Confidential classifications still apply

initiatives.title: Microsoft + UTA Generative AI 'Prompt-A-Thon' and Certified Train-the-Trainer Program
initiatives.description: In May 2024 UTA co-hosted Texas's first Generative AI Prompt-A-Thon with Microsoft, drawing higher-ed and K-12 educators from across the state. UTA simultaneously stood up the country's first certified AI 'train-the-trainer' program, producing a 'Tiger Team' of faculty and staff who then train others on AI tool use.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates UTA's role as a regional hub for AI upskilling and signals deep industry partnership with Microsoft around Copilot and prompt engineering.

investment.funding_source: UTA Offices of Research and Innovation and Information Technology; Microsoft sponsorship
investment.description: Co-hosted event and sustained train-the-trainer program

collaborations.description: Co-hosted Texas's first Prompt-A-Thon; featured Microsoft Education's Chief Innovation Officer MJ Jabbour

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot
tools_and_vendors.role: Featured AI platform for prompt training

initiatives.policy_implications: Reinforces hands-on training as the preferred guardrail for safe GenAI adoption

initiatives.title: $3.1M NIH R01 to Junzhou Huang for AI-Driven Antibody Drug Discovery
initiatives.description: Jenkins Garrett Endowed Professor Junzhou Huang in Computer Science and Engineering won a $3.1M NIH R01 to apply machine learning to antibody drug discovery, extending Rosetta/AlphaFold-style protein-structure prediction to antibody-antigen binding. The team placed first in the international protein contact map prediction track, ahead of Google DeepMind and the University of Washington. A separate $200K Johnson & Johnson award supports AI-based toxicology prediction.
initiatives.significance: One of UTA's most visible AI-for-science grants; cements a growing UT Arlington / UT Southwestern corridor for AI biomedicine.

investment.funding_source: NIH (R01) plus $200K Johnson & Johnson supplemental award
investment.description: Five-year R01 plus industry-sponsored toxicology work

collaborations.description: Experimental validation of computationally predicted antibody structures

tools_and_vendors.name: AlphaFold
tools_and_vendors.role: Reference algorithm being extended for antibody work

initiatives.title: NSF $567K Grant on Securing Natural Language Generation Against Misinformation
initiatives.description: UTA computer science and engineering assistant professor Shirin Nilizadeh won a five-year, $567,609 NSF award to harden NLG systems against misuse that spreads online misinformation. Work examines attacker models, adversarial prompts, and defenses across generative language systems.
initiatives.significance: Positions UTA in the national conversation on AI trust, safety, and misinformation research.

investment.description: Five-year NSF grant on NLG security

initiatives.policy_implications: Builds defensive research capability that feeds UTA's ethical AI guidelines

initiatives.title: CARIDA Center for Artificial Intelligence and Big Data
initiatives.description: Housed in the College of Engineering, CARIDA coordinates UTA research in big data storage and management, AI/ML methods, and applications. Led by Gautam Das (associate dean for research) and Chengkai Li. CARIDA organizes the annual North Texas Symposium on Generative AI (first edition March 2025) and serves as the curriculum advisory body for the MS in Data Science.
initiatives.significance: Provides an intellectual center of gravity for AI research at UTA and a convening platform for DFW higher-ed + industry on generative AI.

investment.funding_source: College of Engineering plus external grant portfolios
investment.description: Umbrella center coordinating multi-faculty AI/ML research

collaborations.description: Industry-facing consultations, symposium sponsorship, and capstone projects

initiatives.title: CloudForce AI Travel Assistant (OIT Innovation)
initiatives.description: An internal AI agent built by UTA's OIT Innovation team that embeds travel policy knowledge plus the GSA Per Diem API so employees can query 'Where can I stay, eat, and expense?' without reading policy PDFs. In testing, targeted launch before Fall 2026.
initiatives.significance: Concrete example of UTA deploying agentic AI against back-office workflows, not just classroom use cases.
initiatives.policy_implications: Validates UTA's intake process for new AI agents

initiatives.title: AI Learning Hub and LinkedIn Learning AI Certification Paths
initiatives.description: UTA publishes a centralized AI Learning Hub that routes faculty and staff into curated LinkedIn Learning paths - Microsoft Azure AI Essentials, Career Essentials in Generative AI, AI for Organizational Leaders, Build Generative AI Productivity Skills (covering ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini, Claude), and role-specific tracks for sales, marketing, recruiting, HR, product, and L&D. Microsoft certification exam costs are included for employees.
initiatives.significance: Operationalizes AI upskilling at scale for a ~7,000-employee workforce and creates visible paths from curiosity to certification.

investment.funding_source: Existing LinkedIn Learning / Microsoft employment benefit

collaborations.description: Pre-built paths and certification exams embedded in employee benefits

initiatives.policy_implications: Ties upskilling to sanctioned vendors, reinforcing UTA's Microsoft-centric AI stack

initiatives.title: Graduate and Non-Credit AI/ML Programs
initiatives.description: UTA offers a credit-bearing Graduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence (four CSE courses stackable toward MS or PhD), an Intelligent Systems concentration within the MS in Computer Science, and, through the EEC Division for Enterprise Development / Pacelearn / WorkForce Institute, an 18-week AI & Machine Learning Bootcamp plus a 14-week 'AI for Software Engineers' bootcamp (priced $4,250-$5,250) that prepares learners for Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate certification.
initiatives.significance: Multi-tier academic-to-workforce pipeline spanning R1 research training and regional non-credit upskilling.

investment.description: Credit and non-credit programs operating at full enrollment

collaborations.description: Non-credit AI/ML bootcamps co-delivered with industry training partners

initiatives.title: AI Webinar Series 'AI in the Classroom: Leadership and Instruction' (College of Education)
initiatives.description: UTA's College of Education launched a public webinar series in April 2026 for K-12 and higher-ed educators covering hands-on machine learning, the PROSE prompt-writing model, AI-enabled lesson design for dual-language STEM classrooms, and responsible AI use. All four presenters are College of Education assistant professors (Lauren Weisberg, Erdogan Kaya, Luis Perez Cortes & Hugh Kellam, Maricela Leon).
initiatives.significance: Extends UTA's AI influence into K-12 and underscores attention to multilingual and equity-aware AI pedagogy.
initiatives.policy_implications: Addresses equity, bias, and data privacy as core pedagogical questions for AI integration

initiatives.title: NSF CAREER Award - AI-Driven Manufacturing Research
initiatives.description: A UTA engineering faculty member won an NSF CAREER Award in July 2025 for approximately $500,000 to advance AI-related manufacturing research, part of a string of federally funded AI work in the College of Engineering.
initiatives.significance: NSF CAREER recognition strengthens UTA's pipeline of early-career AI-in-engineering PIs.

initiatives.title: NSF $600K Machine Learning for Multiscale Materials Modeling
initiatives.description: Xin (Jeffrey) Liu in Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering leads a $600K NSF collaborative research project with Purdue University to use machine learning for modeling and design of advanced composite, metamaterial, soft, and architected materials.
initiatives.significance: Anchors UTA's AI-for-materials agenda and builds R1 cross-institution research muscle.

computing_infrastructure.summary: UTA runs a campus HPC cluster (616 Intel Xeon E5-2699 cores, 3.5 TB RAM, 500 TB storage, 10 Gbps networking to LEARN/Internet2/ESnet on CentOS 7.6) plus a new $2.1M supercomputing research hub launched summer 2025 for AI, biomedical imaging, genomics, and environmental modeling. Exact GPU counts are not publicly disclosed.

regulatory_context.summary: Texas Governor Greg Abbott signed the Texas Responsible Artificial Intelligence Governance Act (TRAIGA, H.B. 149) into law on June 22, 2025; it takes effect January 1, 2026. TRAIGA restricts AI 'restricted purposes' (self-harm, violence, criminality, social scoring by government, political-speech suppression), and applies to developers and deployers doing business in Texas. A December 2025 Trump federal executive order proposes to preempt state AI laws; its practical effect on TRAIGA enforceability is contested. At the system level, the University of Texas System has sponsored AI-across-the-UT-System webinar series, encouraged campus AI strategies, and runs major UT-REAL-Health-AI ($25M) and system-wide Microsoft Copilot agreements that UTA inherits.

narrative_summary: The University of Texas at Arlington is building its AI posture from the middle out - not via a single board-approved AI strategy document, but through a well-coordinated network of policy, research, enablement, and deployment activities. Governance is anchored by an AI Council chaired by CIO Deepika Chalemela, with representation from Research, Legal, Academic Affairs, and the Chief Data and Analytics Officer; a cross-functional AI Advisory Team handles operational detail. OIT's innovation group (visibly represented by Mei Lin, a 2026 DFW AI 75 honoree) is the delivery engine, running the nebulaONE AI teaching assistant pilot across 20+ Fall 2025 courses, shipping a CloudForce travel-policy agent, and maintaining the ai.uta.edu hub. Policy is comprehensive but instructor-flexible: the 2025 GenAI Guidelines for Faculty supply four syllabus language options from prohibited to unrestricted, separate research and staff guidance prohibit confidential data in public tools and require disclosure, and any AI procurement must clear legal, security, audit, and procurement review. Research investment is substantial and growing: a USDA-ARS Smart Agriculture Research Center opened in August 2025 with >$5.5M in connected awards; Junzhou Huang landed a $3.1M NIH R01 for AI antibody drug discovery plus a $200K Johnson & Johnson supplement; Shirin Nilizadeh won a $567K NSF award on NLG security; an NSF CAREER Award and a $600K NSF multiscale-materials collaboration with Purdue round out the pipeline. The university committed $2.1M to a dedicated supercomputing research hub in summer 2025 so AI researchers no longer default to TACC. On the academic side, UTA offers a Graduate Certificate in AI, an Intelligent Systems MSCS concentration, an undergraduate AI certificate, and 14-18 week industry bootcamps via Pacelearn and the WorkForce Institute aimed at Microsoft Azure AI certifications. Enterprise deployment leans heavily Microsoft: free UT System Copilot for all users, paid Copilot for Microsoft 365 via ServiceNow at $360/year, LinkedIn Learning AI paths with included Microsoft exam vouchers. Gaps: UTA has not appointed a dedicated Chief AI Officer, the central AI hub does not yet publish a unified strategic plan, predictive analytics for retention and admissions AI are not visibly documented, and the Texas Responsible AI Governance Act's effect on procurement and operations is still working through the system. Overall UTA looks like a competent, Microsoft-aligned R1 moving quickly on faculty enablement and sponsored research while keeping student-facing AI carefully piloted.
